response:
to solve this problem we must first store the concept of prime factors in our mind.
***
the number one hundred factorial contains many factors of two and five to store.
***
we need to store the count of prime factors five in one hundred factorial.
***
the count of factors of five is twenty four when we store the sum of quotients.
***
the power of ten is fifty so we store the difference between two and five counts.
***
since there are more twos than fives the denominator will store only the remaining fives.
***
we calculate twenty four minus fifty to store the net power of five in the denominator.
***
this calculation shows a negative exponent meaning the fraction simplifies to an integer.
***
when a fraction reduces to an integer the denominator must store the value one.
***
the final answer is one because we have stored all necessary calculations correctly.
